backup.heading: Database Backup & Restore
backup.local_database_warning: Backup and restore is designed for remote databases (libsql://). You are currently using a local database.
backup.storage_not_configured: Storage is not configured. Backups require Bunny CDN or local storage to be enabled.
backup.encryption_key_description: You will need this key to restore a backup to a different site. Store it securely — it cannot be recovered.
backup.create_backup_description: Creates a .zip archive containing a .sql file for each database table. Backups are not encrypted (the sensitive contents are already encrypted at the field level).
backup.restore_warning: Warning: Restoring will delete all current data and replace it with the backup contents. This cannot be undone.
backup.retention_count: {count, plural, one {There is 1 backup} other {There are # backups}}
backup.retention_kept: , shown newest first. Up to {maxBackups} are kept —
backup.retention_remaining: {remaining} more can be created before the oldest is purged.
backup.retention_purge: the next will purge the oldest ({oldest}).
backup.restore_confirm_phrase: This will restore my whole database to an earlier state. Existing info will be lost. I understand that this is dangerous.
backup.schema_mismatch_warning: Schema mismatch: This backup was created with a different database schema version. The restore will apply current migrations after importing data, but some data may be incompatible.
backup.restore_confirmation_intro: You are about to restore from an uploaded backup containing {lineCount} SQL statements. This will:
backup.restore_step_import_data: Import all data from the backup
